title: potentilla patula subsp. tenella
intro: Potentilla patula subsp. tenella, also known as small cinquefoil, is a flowering plant species in the family Rosaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and tannins. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
